source_pr.title: planner: fix bug that skew distinct agg rule failed to generate plan
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?
Previously when the group aggregation doesn't output the grouping columns, the skew distinct agg rewrite rule will fail to generate a plan. This patch fixes the bug.

Issue Number: close #36350

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests 

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note


```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select count(distinct b), sum(c) from t group by a order by 1,2;").Check( (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select count(distinct b), sum(c) from t group by a order by 1,2;").Check( (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select count(distinct b), sum(c) from t group by a order by 1,2;").Check( (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)
